ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302114 covers sesame ball as a food product; the specific black sugar and cashew nut variant is not individually coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers fried dough products; no specific code exists for glutinous rice sesame balls. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific dim sum dessert item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 pieces of deep-fried sesame ball, approximately 120 g total, as sold at Loong Dim Sum and similar Cantonese dim sum restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Jian Dui / Sesame Ball — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table, supplemented by component-level estimation for black sugar and cashew nut variant
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Deep-Fried Sesame Ball with Black Sugar and Cashew Nut' exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for glutinous rice flour (FDC ID 169717), sesame seeds (FDC ID 12023), cashew nuts (FDC ID 12087), and brown sugar (FDC ID 169655), combined in proportions consistent with standard jian dui recipes (approximately 55% glutinous rice dough, 20% filling of black sugar and cashew, 10% sesame seed coating, 15% absorbed frying oil). Values are cross-referenced against FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table entries for similar fried glutinous rice products. Per-100g values represent the finished fried product.

gi_source.title: Glutinous rice-based fried confection — analogous estimate based on 'Rice cake, glutinous, fried' and 'Sesame ball' GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for deep-fried sesame ball with black sugar and cashew nut. GI estimated at 61 (medium) based on: (1) glutinous rice flour is the dominant ingredient and has a GI of approximately 65–75 when cooked; (2) deep-frying and the presence of fat and protein from cashew nuts and sesame seeds moderates the glycemic response, reducing effective GI to approximately 61; (3) analogous fried glutinous rice products in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al. (2008) 'International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008', Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283, support a medium GI range for this food category. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 69.6) / 100 ≈ 42.5, rounded to 43.0. Serving carbohydrate = 58.0 g/100g × 120 g serving = 69.6 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~85 mg/100g); low saturated fat
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 102 mg sodium per 3-piece serving, deep-fried sesame balls are relatively low in sodium and do not pose a significant hypertension risk when consumed occasionally, though the high caloric density from frying warrants portion control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load from glutinous rice flour and black sugar; medium GI (61); high glycemic load per serving (~43)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of deep-fried sesame balls due to the high glycemic load (~43 per 3-piece serving) driven by glutinous rice flour and black sugar filling, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion should be restricted to 1 piece and paired with low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate total fat from deep-frying; low saturated fat; zero cholesterol; beneficial unsaturated fats from sesame and cashew
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While deep-frying contributes to total fat content (~17.4 g per serving), the fat profile is predominantly unsaturated (sesame and cashew-derived), cholesterol is absent, and saturated fat is low (~3.4 g per serving), making occasional consumption acceptable within a heart-healthy diet when overall energy intake is managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Deep-Fried Sesame Ball with Black Sugar and Cashew Nut (known in Cantonese as 煎堆, jian dui) is a classic Chinese dim sum dessert consisting of a hollow sphere of deep-fried glutinous rice dough coated generously with white sesame seeds and filled with a sweet mixture of black sugar (a dark, molasses-rich sugar) and crushed or whole cashew nuts. The exterior is crisp and golden-brown with a satisfying crunch from the toasted sesame coating, while the interior is chewy, elastic, and slightly sticky — characteristic of glutinous rice flour — with a rich, caramel-like sweetness from the black sugar filling and a buttery nuttiness from the cashews. The dish is served hot, typically in portions of 3 pieces, and is a staple of Cantonese yum cha (dim sum) culture, enjoyed across Singapore, Hong Kong, and southern China. Nutritionally, each 3-piece serving (~120 g) provides approximately 462 kcal, 69.6 g carbohydrate, 17.4 g fat, and 7.4 g protein. The glycemic load is high (~43 per serving), making this a high-impact food for blood glucose management. The fat profile is predominantly unsaturated due to sesame and cashew content. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycemic load, and should be consumed in strict moderation. It is vegetarian, vegan, dairy-free, and halal-suitable, but contains sesame and tree nut (cashew) allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific variant (black sugar and cashew nut filling) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from component-level USDA SR Legacy data for glutinous rice flour, sesame seeds, cashew nuts, and brown sugar in proportions consistent with standard jian dui recipes. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for glutinous rice-based fried products. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-level estimates; no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific black sugar and cashew nut sesame ball variant. Values should be verified against laboratory analysis or a future HPB Singapore Foodo entry if one becomes available. 2. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): this is a plant-based fried product with no animal-derived ingredients; cholesterol is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for all component ingredients. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0 (confirmed zero): glutinous rice flour, sesame seeds, cashew nuts, and black sugar contain negligible or zero vitamin A; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy component data. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): this is a vegan plant-based product with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero): no animal-derived or fortified ingredients are present; vitamin D is naturally zero per component data. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and are approximate. 7. GI of 61 is an analogous estimate; direct measurement for this specific product has not been published. 8. The restaurant name 'Loong Dim Sum - North Canal Road' and menu name 'The Bom' are preserved from the field survey data source record for traceability. 9. Serving size of 120 g (3 pieces) is estimated based on typical jian dui portion weights at Singaporean dim sum restaurants; actual weight may vary by restaurant.
